content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Verified both amendments. fr-16-cc963f89: reproduced the failing test via `cargo test --features test-stub` — the Claude-variant sidecar recovery test writes a Claude transcript but the initial run used the default Codex implementer, so the persisted `stage_target` drives resume to decode with Codex (iterative_resume_target at engine.rs:6264 ignores the refreshed config), producing a schema-validation failure. flake.nix:50-64 feeds the same test flags, so `nix build` is blocked. fr-16-f519c131: confirmed by reading the code. carry_forward_iterative_state only retains iterative state when cursor.attempt matches, cursor.retry() always increments attempt, and the retry path at engine.rs:6034 re-enters carry_forward_active_run, so a transient retryable class (transport up to 5 attempts) resets completed_iterations/stable_count to zero, letting the loop do up to max_rounds *additional* iterations and clobbering an almost-converged stable streak.

## Votes

- `fr-16-cc963f89`: Accept
  Reproduced by running `cargo test --features test-stub --test unit iterative_minimal_resume_recovers_terminal_claude_raw_output_when_sidecar_is_missing`; it panics with a `missing field change_summary` schema-validation error on the recovered iteration. Root cause matches the finding: the first run uses the Codex default (config.rs:1549-1550), persists `stage_target=codex` in the iterative implementer state after iteration 3, the test then rewrites the .raw file as a Claude envelope and flips the backend config to Claude, but resume routes through iterative_resume_target (engine.rs:6259-6281) which deliberately prefers the persisted target over the refreshed config — so the Claude transcript is parsed with the Codex decoder and fails validation. Because flake.nix:50-64 sets `cargoTestFlags = ["--features","test-stub"]` on both static and dynamic Nix builds, this failure blocks `nix build`, which is the bead's required verification gate. Fix aligns with the remediation hint (set implementer_backend=claude before the first run, mirroring the Codex-variant sibling test at line 2413).
- `fr-16-f519c131`: Accept
  Confirmed by reading the code. engine.rs:7268-7283 persists completed_iterations/stable_count after each successful iteration, but carry_forward_iterative_state (engine.rs:747-756) only returns the prior state when stage, cycle, attempt, AND completion_round all match. The retry path always advances attempt via `cursor = cursor.retry()?` (engine.rs:6034) which increments attempt by 1 (domain.rs:860-870), and the next loop iteration rebuilds active_run through carry_forward_active_run at engine.rs:5730 — so the iterative state is discarded on every retry. Retry rules permit up to 5 attempts on TransportFailure and 3 on SchemaValidation/Timeout (retry_policy.rs:54-63), so a single late-loop transient failure can push total iterations well beyond max_consecutive_implementer_rounds (15 done + up to 20 more with default cap) and wipes an almost-converged stable streak. That's a real cost/runtime defect and a silent cap violation regardless of whether it has fired in the wild yet. Remediation hint is appropriate: carry iterative state across retry attempts within the same stage/cycle/completion_round, or at minimum subtract already-completed iterations from the remaining budget.
